overview.location: Dania Beach, Broward County, Florida — 3 miles southwest of downtown Fort Lauderdale, 21 miles north of downtown Miami
overview.distinction: South Florida's second-busiest airport and a major gateway for low-cost carriers. Spirit Airlines' largest hub. Key airport for cruise passengers heading to Port Everglades. Known as the budget-friendly alternative to MIA for South Florida travel. Serves the Greater Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, and northern Miami-Dade market. Over 700 daily flights to 140+ destinations across the U.S., Latin America, Caribbean, and Europe.
overview.terminalUnderConstruction: Terminal 5 (5 gates, ~230,000 sq ft, $404M, completion mid-2026)

runwayDetails.note: Unique sloped runway that rises gradually from west to east, passing over US-1 highway via a retaining wall. Extended in 2014 as part of the Airport Expansion Program.

overview.formerRunway: Runway 13/31 (crosswind) — closed and decommissioned in 2013 to allow expansion of 10R/28L
overview.dominantAirline: Spirit Airlines (largest hub — operates from Terminal 4)
overview.cruisePortProximity: Port Everglades is 2 miles east of FLL — one of the busiest cruise ports in the world. FLL is the primary airport for cruise passengers.
overview.brightlineNote: Brightline high-speed rail station in downtown Fort Lauderdale (4 miles from FLL) connects to Miami, West Palm Beach, and Orlando. Brightline Airport Connector shuttle available from terminal.

terminals.name: Terminal 1 (Yellow Terminal) — Concourses A, B, C
terminals.status: Active — 24 gates across 3 concourses. Serves low-cost and domestic carriers.
terminals.gates: A1-A7 (Concourse A), B1-B9 (Concourse B), C1-C9 (Concourse C)
terminals.notes: Largest terminal by gate count. United Club lounge in Concourse C. Concourse A on east side, B in center, C on west side. TSA PreCheck and CLEAR available.

terminals.name: Terminal 2 (Red Terminal) — Concourse D
terminals.status: Active — 9 gates. Recently modernized by Delta Air Lines.
terminals.notes: Smallest terminal. Delta Sky Club lounge between Gates D2 and D3. Delta completed a terminal modernization project. Located at the northwest end of the airport.

terminals.name: Terminal 3 (Purple Terminal) — Concourses E, F
terminals.status: Active — 20 gates. Mix of domestic and international carriers.
terminals.notes: Escape Lounge - The Centurion Studio Partner located in this terminal. American Airlines and JetBlue are the primary carriers. Located at the far west end of the airport.

terminals.name: Terminal 4 (Green Terminal) — Concourse G
terminals.status: Active — 14 gates. Spirit Airlines hub terminal.
terminals.notes: Spirit Airlines' largest hub operation. Terminal 5 (under construction, mid-2026 completion) will connect to Terminal 4 via multi-level pedestrian bridge. Primarily serves JetBlue overflow and Spirit.

transit: Broward County Transit (BCT) Bus Route 1 (US-1 corridor)
Tri-Rail (Fort Lauderdale station — 3 miles north, shuttle/bus connection)
Brightline high-speed rail (Fort Lauderdale station — 4 miles north, Airport Connector shuttle available)
Miami-Dade Transit (connections via Tri-Rail/Brightline to Miami)

taxi.pickup: Lower level (Arrivals) of each terminal at designated taxi stands
taxi.note: Metered fares. Licensed taxis available 24/7 at arrivals level of all terminals. Cash and credit cards accepted.

pickupLocations: Between Terminals 1 and 2 on innermost curb
Between Terminals 3 and 4 on innermost curb (Palm Garage 1st floor, Zones A-D)
rideshare.surgePricing: Standard surge pricing during spring break, cruise ship embarkation days, holidays, and Fort Lauderdale Boat Show
rideshare.tip: Going to the upper level (Departures) to request your ride can save time — lighter traffic flow than arrivals level.
rideshare.note: Rideshare is generally cheaper than metered taxi from FLL, especially for longer distances. Pickup zones clearly marked at GTA-2 (Terminals 1-2) and Palm Garage ground floor (Terminals 3-4).

rentalCars.location: Rental Car Center (RCC) — accessible via free shuttle from lower level (Arrivals) of each terminal
rentalCars.note: All rental car companies are consolidated at the RCC, located south of the airport. Free shuttle from arrivals level. Future plans include an automated people mover to replace shuttle buses.

brightline.description: High-speed inter-city rail at Fort Lauderdale station (downtown, 4 miles from airport). Airport Connector shuttle available.
brightline.shuttleFare: $12 per guest with same-day Brightline ticket
brightline.shuttlePickup: Lower level Arrivals roadway at GTA-1, GTA-2, and GTA-3
brightline.destinations: Miami (30 min) ↔ Fort Lauderdale ↔ West Palm Beach (45 min) ↔ Orlando (3 hours)
brightline.note: Brightline is the fastest ground option to Miami (~30 min) and Orlando (~3 hours). The $12 Airport Connector shuttle links FLL to the Fort Lauderdale Brightline station downtown.

triRail.description: Commuter rail connecting Miami, Fort Lauderdale, and West Palm Beach along the I-95 corridor
triRail.nearestStation: Fort Lauderdale station (3 miles north of airport) — accessible via BCT bus or rideshare
triRail.coverage: 18 stations from Miami Airport to Mangonia Park (West Palm Beach)
triRail.note: Tri-Rail does not have a direct airport station at FLL. Take BCT bus Route 40 or a rideshare to the Fort Lauderdale Tri-Rail station. Future Intermodal Center at FLL will include direct Tri-Rail access.

bus.note: BCT buses connect FLL to surrounding neighborhoods. Route 40 connects to the Tri-Rail Fort Lauderdale station.

groundTransportation.hotelShuttles: Many nearby hotels offer complimentary shuttle service — check with your hotel before booking. Hotels along US-1, I-95, and near Port Everglades commonly provide airport shuttles.

parkingRates: On-airport: Short-term (Hibiscus Level 2, Palm Level 1) $36/day, Long-term (Hibiscus Levels 3-7, Palm Levels 2-4, Cypress Levels 7-9) $20/day, Valet $30/day, Overflow $10/day. Off-site: from ~$5-8/day with shuttle. Pre-booking recommended during peak season.

hibiscusGarage.name: Hibiscus Garage
hibiscusGarage.serves: Terminal 1 (Concourses A, B, C) and Terminal 2 (Concourse D)
hibiscusGarage.features: Connected to terminals via covered walkway. Short-term on Level 2, long-term on Levels 3-7. Accessible parking available. Automated payment systems.
hibiscusGarage.note: Closest garage to Terminals 1 and 2. Short-term Level 2 is ideal for pickups/drop-offs.

palmGarage.name: Palm Garage
palmGarage.serves: Terminal 3 (Concourses E, F) and Terminal 4 (Concourse G)
palmGarage.evCharging: EV charging stations and tire inflation stations available
palmGarage.features: Parksmart-certified green garage. EV charging, tire inflation. Connected to terminals via covered walkway. Rideshare pickup area on Level 1 (Zones A-D) for Terminals 3-4. Free inter-terminal shuttle stops here.
palmGarage.note: Serves Terminals 3 and 4. Rideshare (Uber/Lyft) pickup zone for Terminals 3-4 is on Level 1.

cypressGarage.name: Cypress Garage
cypressGarage.serves: All terminals via free shuttle/walkway connection
cypressGarage.features: Parksmart-certified green garage. Electric vehicle charging, tire inflation, electric utility vehicle fleet. Free shuttle to terminals and downtown Fort Lauderdale. Sustainability-focused design. Will connect to Terminal 5 via pedestrian bridge upon T5 completion.
cypressGarage.note: Furthest garage from terminals but connected via free shuttle. Long-term only on upper levels. Best budget on-airport option at $20/day.

valetParking.name: Valet Parking
valetParking.features: Premium curbside drop-off. Available at departures level. Most convenient option for quick airport access.

overflowParking.name: Overflow Parking Lot
overflowParking.availability: Only opens during peak travel periods when garages are full
overflowParking.note: Cheapest on-airport option but only available during busy periods (holidays, spring break, cruise embarkation days). Cannot be relied upon for regular travel.

cellPhoneLot.name: Cell Phone Waiting Lot
cellPhoneLot.location: Perimeter Road, south of the airport
cellPhoneLot.rules: For passenger pickup only. Driver must remain with vehicle. Monitor flight status and proceed to terminal when passenger is ready.

evCharging.totalPorts: 30+ EV charging ports across all garages
evCharging.note: All three garages have EV charging capability. Palm and Cypress garages are Parksmart-certified green garages with additional sustainability features.

onAirportParking.accessibleParking: ADA-compliant accessible parking spaces available on all garage levels closest to terminal connections. Van-accessible spaces available.
onAirportParking.totalCapacity: ~11,000 vehicles across all garages and lots
onAirportParking.paymentMethods: Cash, Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, Apple Pay, contactless payment. Automated pay stations in garages.
onAirportParking.garageServiceAreas: Hibiscus → Terminals 1-2. Palm → Terminals 3-4. Cypress → All terminals (via shuttle). Free inter-terminal Breeze shuttle connects all terminals.

parkingLots.name: Park By The Ports (FLL)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free 24/7 shuttle to FLL terminals, approximately every 10-15 minutes
parkingLots.overview: Popular off-site lot about 5 minutes from FLL. Also serves Port Everglades cruise passengers. Free 24/7 shuttle service with frequent departures.
parkingLots.notes: Dual-purpose lot serving both airport and cruise port travelers. 24/7 shuttle is reliable. Good value at $7.99/day compared to $20-36 on-airport.

parkingLots.name: Tropical Airport Parking at Homewood Suites (FLL)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle to FLL terminals, runs every 15-20 minutes
parkingLots.overview: Off-site parking at Homewood Suites hotel just 1.4 miles from FLL. One of the closest off-site options. No hotel stay required.
parkingLots.notes: Closest off-site lot to FLL. Hotel lot means well-maintained and lit. Pre-book online for the $6.95 rate — walk-up is $8.95/day.

parkingLots.name: Self Park FLL
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle service to FLL terminals
parkingLots.overview: Established off-site parking provider near FLL. Pre-book online for discounted rate. Free shuttle included.
parkingLots.notes: Well-known local provider. Note: lot may undergo construction/relocation — check website for current status before booking.

parkingLots.name: FastTrack Fort Lauderdale Airport Parking
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle running every 10-15 minutes to FLL terminals
parkingLots.overview: Off-site parking near FLL with both self-park and valet options. Frequent shuttle service. Competitive pre-booking rate.
parkingLots.notes: Valet option is convenient for travelers in a rush. Pre-book online for best rate ($6.99 vs $9.95 walk-up).

parkingLots.name: Gold Coast FLL Airport Parking
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle to FLL terminals, on-demand service
parkingLots.overview: Off-site parking near Fort Lauderdale airport with on-demand shuttle service. Budget-friendly rates with pre-booking discounts.
parkingLots.notes: On-demand shuttle means less waiting. Fenced and monitored lot. Pre-book for $7.49 vs $9.95 walk-up.

parkingLots.name: Best Western Plus Fort Lauderdale Airport & Cruise Port (FLL)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle to FLL terminals, hourly schedule
parkingLots.overview: Hotel parking near FLL and Port Everglades. No hotel stay required for parking. Gated and secure lot with hotel-grade maintenance.
parkingLots.notes: Also serves Port Everglades cruise passengers. Hourly shuttle — plan accordingly for early/late flights. Gated lot adds security.

parkingLots.name: Rodeway Inn & Suites FLL Airport Parking
parkingLots.overview: Budget hotel parking option near FLL. Among the cheapest off-site rates when pre-booked at $5.95/day.
parkingLots.notes: Best budget off-site option at $5.95/day pre-booked. Basic lot but gets the job done. Verify shuttle hours before booking for early/late flights.

shuttleInfo: On-airport: Free Breeze shuttle connects all four terminals. Free shuttle from Cypress Garage to terminals. Rental Car Center shuttle from arrivals level. Off-site: Most lots include free shuttle — frequency ranges from every 10 minutes (Park By The Ports, FastTrack) to hourly (Best Western). Brightline Airport Connector shuttle available from arrivals level ($12 with same-day Brightline ticket). CRITICAL: Verify shuttle hours for early morning and late night flights — some off-site lots have limited shuttle schedules.
distanceFromCity: 3 miles southwest of downtown Fort Lauderdale. 10-15 minutes by car. 21 miles north of downtown Miami. South Florida's budget-friendly airport alternative to MIA.

keyDistances.toDowntownMiami: 28 miles, 30-45 min by car, $30-50 rideshare, ~30 min Brightline
keyDistances.toMIAAirport: 26 miles, 25-40 min by car, Tri-Rail or Brightline available
keyDistances.toWestPalmBeach: 45 miles, 45-60 min by car, ~45 min Brightline, Tri-Rail available
keyDistances.toOrlando: 200 miles, 3-3.5 hours by car, ~3 hours Brightline
keyDistances.toKeyWest: 185 miles, 4-4.5 hours by car (Overseas Highway via Miami)
keyDistances.competitiveAdvantage: FLL's proximity to Port Everglades (2 miles) makes it the #1 airport for cruise passengers — a $10 taxi ride vs $55+ from MIA. For downtown Fort Lauderdale, a $12-20 rideshare beats airport parking for any trip over 2-3 days. Brightline connects to Miami in 30 min and Orlando in 3 hours from the nearby Fort Lauderdale station.

construction2026.status: Major expansion underway — $3.2 billion capital improvement program.
projects: Terminal 5 ($404M) — 5 new domestic gates, 230,000 sq ft, two-level terminal. Multi-level pedestrian bridge connecting to Terminal 4 and Cypress Garage. Expected to accommodate 5 million additional passengers annually. Completion mid-2026.

Terminal Modernization Program — Facility-wide renovations to all terminals including interior finishes, restrooms, hold rooms, security checkpoints, airside connectors, signage/wayfinding, and concessions.
Airside Terminal Connections — Construction of airside connections between Terminals 1, 2, and 3 (currently passengers must exit security to switch terminals).
Automated People Mover — Planned system connecting all terminals, garages, and future intermodal center. Will replace current shuttle buses.
Palm Garage Reconstruction — Existing Palm Garage to be reconstructed with adjacent on-site hotel and conference center.
Terminal 3 Rebuild & Expansion — Complete rebuild and expansion of Terminal 3.
Intermodal Center — Future transit hub with direct Tri-Rail and potential Broward Commuter Rail (FEC Coastal Link) access.
Concessions Master Plan — Airport-wide food, beverage, and retail upgrades across all terminals.
construction2026.travelImpact: Terminal 5 construction is the most active project. Airport remains fully operational throughout. Some terminal areas may have temporary detours. No major parking closures expected in 2026.

tsaPreCheck.note: TSA PreCheck dedicated lanes available at all four terminal security checkpoints. Average PreCheck wait: ~16 minutes vs ~31 minutes standard.

clear.note: CLEAR lanes available at all terminals. CLEAR members verify identity at kiosks, then escorted to front of security line. Combined with PreCheck for fastest experience.

globalEntry.note: Global Entry kiosks available in international arrivals areas. Mobile Passport Control (MPC) also available.

standardWaitTimes.tips: FLL can have significant security waits during winter tourist season and spring break. Best times: early morning (before 6 AM) and mid-afternoon (2-4 PM).

arrivalRecommendation.holidays: 3+ hours during spring break, Thanksgiving, Christmas, and cruise embarkation days

securityAndScreening.checkpointHours: Checkpoint hours vary by terminal — check fly-fll.com or the Broward County Aviation Department website for current hours.

terminal1.name: United Club
terminal1.access: United Club membership, United or Star Alliance premium cabin, Star Alliance Gold, one-day pass ($59)
terminal1.amenities: Complimentary food, drinks, bar, WiFi, workspaces, power outlets.

terminal2.name: Delta Sky Club
terminal2.location: Terminal 2, between Gates D2 and D3
terminal2.access: Delta Sky Club membership, Delta One, Delta Reserve Amex, SkyTeam Elite Plus, Amex Platinum (limited visits)
terminal2.amenities: Full-service bar, complimentary food, shower facilities, WiFi, workspaces, power outlets.

terminal3.name: Escape Lounge - The Centurion Studio Partner
terminal3.access: Amex Platinum/Centurion cardholders (free), Priority Pass members, paid walk-in ($45-55)
terminal3.amenities: Premium food, cocktails, full bar, WiFi, power outlets. Only lounge at FLL open to walk-in guests and Priority Pass.
terminal3.note: The only lounge at FLL accessible via Priority Pass or paid walk-in. Best option for travelers without airline status.

terminal4.name: No dedicated lounge
terminal4.note: Terminal 4 (Spirit Airlines hub) does not currently have a lounge. Nearest lounge is the Escape Lounge in Terminal 3 — but requires exiting security and re-screening if connecting from Terminal 4.

lounges.importantNote: FLL's lounge options are limited compared to MIA. There are no Priority Pass lounges outside of the Escape Lounge in Terminal 3. Travelers in Terminal 4 (Spirit) have no lounge access. The Escape Lounge in Concourse B was closed for renovation from October 2025 through end of 2026.

wifi.note: Free unlimited WiFi in all terminals. Performance may decrease during peak congestion hours.

evCharging.totalPorts: 30+ EV charging ports across all garages
evCharging.note: All three garages (Hibiscus, Palm, Cypress) have EV charging stations. Palm and Cypress garages are Parksmart-certified green garages.

luggageStorage.onSite: Bags to Go luggage storage service available in all four terminals.

details.locations: All four terminals (Terminal 1: 2nd Floor near Concourse B exit)
details.limitation: Limited hours — closes by 5:30-6:30 PM. Not available for late-night flights.

petFacilities.total: 10 pet relief areas — 6 indoor Service Animal Relief Areas (SARAs) and 4 outdoor pet relief areas
petFacilities.indoorLocations: 6 indoor SARAs located inside the secure areas of the terminals
petFacilities.outdoorLocations: 4 outdoor pet relief areas on the lower level (arrivals), outside each baggage claim area: outside Terminal 1 under the garage walkway, outside Palm Garage across from Terminal 2, across from Terminal 3, and across from Terminal 4
petFacilities.amenities: Complimentary waste disposal bags provided at all locations
petFacilities.note: FLL has extensive pet relief options with both indoor (post-security) and outdoor (pre-security) facilities at every terminal.

peakTravelTimes.seasonalPeak: November-April is peak season (snowbird season — massive influx of northeastern travelers). Spring break (March-April), Thanksgiving, Christmas/New Year, and cruise embarkation days at Port Everglades create major spikes. Fort Lauderdale International Boat Show (October) also drives significant traffic.
peakTravelTimes.cruiseImpact: Port Everglades cruise ship turnaround days (typically Saturday/Sunday) cause surges in both passenger volume and ground transportation demand. Parking garages can fill up on heavy cruise embarkation days.

arrivalRecommendation.holidays: 3+ hours during peak season (November-April), spring break, and cruise days

weatherPatterns.climate: Tropical rainforest climate. Hot and humid year-round. Wet season May-October. Hurricane season June-November.
weatherPatterns.commonDisruptions: Hurricane season (June-November) is the primary disruption risk. Afternoon thunderstorms year-round but especially June-September. Heavy rain can cause temporary ground stops and flight delays.
weatherPatterns.bestMonths: November through April — dry season, comfortable temperatures, but busier airport due to snowbird season
weatherPatterns.worstMonths: August-October (peak hurricane risk + extreme humidity + daily afternoon thunderstorms)
weatherPatterns.hurricaneNote: FLL is in a major hurricane zone. The airport has been shut down multiple times during hurricanes (notably Hurricane Irma in 2017). Travel insurance strongly recommended for June-November travel. Airport has robust recovery procedures but cancellations are inevitable during named storms.
weatherPatterns.floodingNote: FLL experienced significant flooding in April 2023 that temporarily closed the airport. The Fort Lauderdale area is susceptible to urban flooding during extreme rainfall events.
weatherPatterns.summerThunderstorms: June-September: daily afternoon thunderstorms are nearly guaranteed. Typically 2-5 PM. Usually clear by evening. Can cause 1-2 hour delays but rarely last all day.
weatherPatterns.note: Morning flights are most reliable year-round. FLL's tropical location means weather disruptions are common but usually brief (except hurricanes and extreme flooding events).

parkSleepFly.description: Hotel + parking + shuttle packages near FLL. Popular with both airport travelers and Port Everglades cruise passengers.
parkSleepFly.priceRange: From ~$99/night (1-night stay + up to 14-21 days parking + free airport shuttle)

popularHotels.name: Courtyard by Marriott Fort Lauderdale Airport & Cruise Port
popularHotels.highlight: Close to both airport and Port Everglades. Popular with cruise travelers.

popularHotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Fort Lauderdale Airport
Sleep Inn & Suites Fort Lauderdale International Airport
Hampton Inn & Suites Fort Lauderdale Airport
Residence Inn Fort Lauderdale Airport & Cruise Port
popularHotels.highlight: Closest hotel to FLL. Suite-style rooms good for families.

rideshareRoundTrip.note: Cheaper than parking for trips of any length if headed to downtown Fort Lauderdale

brightlineToMiami.note: Fastest ground option to/from Miami — 30 min train ride

savingsComparison.keyDifference: FLL off-site parking offers excellent value — $5.95-7.99/day with shuttle vs $20-36/day on-airport. The overflow lot at $10/day is the cheapest on-airport option but only opens during peak periods. For downtown Fort Lauderdale travelers, a rideshare at $12-20 beats even off-site parking for trips over 2 days. Port Everglades cruise passengers benefit from park-by-the-ports options serving both airport and cruise port.

uniqueFeatures.budgetCarrierHub: FLL is South Florida's budget airline capital. Spirit Airlines' largest hub, plus major focus city operations for Frontier, JetBlue, and Southwest. Often significantly cheaper flights than MIA for domestic travel.
uniqueFeatures.cruisePortProximity: Port Everglades — one of the world's busiest cruise ports — is just 2 miles from FLL. A $10 taxi ride vs $55+ from MIA makes FLL the natural choice for cruise passengers. Many off-site parking lots serve both the airport and the cruise port.
uniqueFeatures.slopedRunway: Runway 10R/28L has a unique engineering feature — it rises gradually from west to east and passes over US-1 highway via a massive retaining wall. One of the most unusual runway designs at any major U.S. airport.
uniqueFeatures.terminal5Coming: Terminal 5 ($404M, mid-2026) will be the first new terminal in decades — adding 5 gates and 5 million annual passenger capacity. Represents the airport's transformation to handle growing South Florida demand.
uniqueFeatures.snowbirdGateway: FLL is the primary arrival point for 'snowbirds' — seasonal residents from the Northeast and Midwest who winter in South Florida. November-April is peak season with massive passenger volume increases.
uniqueFeatures.fllVsMia: FLL is the budget-friendly alternative to MIA (26 miles south). MIA dominates international travel (90+ airlines, 195 destinations) while FLL excels at domestic low-cost flights and cruise port convenience. Many savvy travelers fly into FLL for domestic trips even when staying in Miami — saving on both airfare and parking.
uniqueFeatures.fortLauderdaleBoatShow: The Fort Lauderdale International Boat Show (October) is the world's largest in-water boat show, driving significant airport traffic and filling nearby hotels and parking.
uniqueFeatures.brightlineAccess: Brightline high-speed rail from the nearby Fort Lauderdale station connects to Miami (30 min), West Palm Beach (45 min), and Orlando (3 hours) — making FLL a viable airport for a much larger geographic area.
uniqueFeatures.portEvergladesEconomy: Port Everglades handles 4+ million cruise passengers annually plus major cargo operations. The port's economic activity drives significant ground transportation demand at FLL.

liveSources._description: Authoritative URLs for article generation. Verified 2026-04-09.
